name: Whole Wheat Buttermilk Pancakes
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Buttermilk -or- Sour Milk (1 cup lowfat milk + 1 tablespoon vinegar)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, stir together the whole wheat flour, unbleached white flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
2. In another bowl, beat the egg, buttermilk or sour milk mixture, and vegetable oil until well combined.
3. Gradually add the liquid ingredients to the dry ingredients, stirring just until blended (do not overmix).
4. Heat a large griddle or non-stick skillet over medium heat. Grease lightly with cooking spray or butter.
5. Pour about 1/4 cup batter onto the hot griddle for each pancake. Cook until bubbles form on the surface and the edges look set, about 2-3 minutes.
6. Flip the pancakes and cook the other side until golden brown, about 1-2 minutes more.
7. Serve the pancakes warm with fresh fruit slices, unsweetened applesauce, or a low-calorie syrup.
Makes 12 4-inch pancakes
Two Pancakes = Calories: 114 Carbohydrates: 18 Protein: 4 Fat: 3 Sodium: 246 Potassium: 1200 Cholesterol: 47 Exchange Value: 1 Bread Exchange + 1/2 Fat Exchange
